ModuleName: SQL Joins, Relationships, and Advanced Query Techniques

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline listing the module's core topics—relational database foundations, INNER and OUTER JOINs, and subqueries—used by instructors to plan lessons and by learners to navigate what will be covered.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: This navigation page outlines the module's topics — relational database foundations, JOIN types, subqueries, aggregation, and multi-table problem-solving — giving learners and instructors a structured overview of what the module covers and in what order.

Artifacts.Name: SQL_Joins_Relationships_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing SQL joins, relationships, and advanced query techniques—covering relational database concepts, JOIN types, subqueries, and aggregation—used by instructors to deliver lectures or by learners to review key concepts and examples.

Artifacts.Name: Relational Database Foundations
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces relational database concepts including the mathematical basis of relations, how tables are structured, and how foreign keys link tables together, serving as foundational background before learners work with JOIN operations.

Artifacts.Name: INNER JOIN and Matching Records
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how INNER JOIN works to combine rows from multiple tables based on matching key values, covering syntax, filtering logic, and practical business query examples so learners can retrieve related data across tables.

Artifacts.Name: Outer JOINs: LEFT, RIGHT, and FULL
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers LEFT, RIGHT, and FULL OUTER JOIN operations, explaining when and why each is used to retain unmatched rows from one or both tables, with examples that contrast their behavior against INNER JOIN.

Artifacts.Name: Subqueries and Nested SELECT Statements
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how to write and use subqueries — SELECT statements nested inside other queries — covering scalar, row, and table subqueries as well as correlated subqueries, so learners can build complex multi-step filtering and comparison logic.

Artifacts.Name: Aggregation with GROUP BY and HAVING
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers SQL aggregation using GROUP BY to segment data into logical groups and HAVING to filter those groups, explaining aggregate functions such as COUNT, SUM, and AVG so learners can produce summary statistics from large datasets.

Artifacts.Name: Solving Multi-Table Analytical Problems
Artifacts.Description: This reading walks learners through a structured methodology for tackling complex analytical questions that span multiple tables, combining JOINs, subqueries, and aggregation into cohesive queries that address real-world business scenarios.

Artifacts.Name: Solving Multi-Table Analytical Problems
Artifacts.Description: This reading walks learners through a structured methodology for tackling complex analytical questions that span multiple tables, combining JOINs, subqueries, and aggregation into cohesive queries that address real-world business scenarios.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question quiz bank assessing learners' understanding of relational database concepts, primary and foreign keys, JOIN behavior, and related query logic, used by instructors to build graded assessments or knowledge checks.
